key.name: color-scheme
key.description: Whether viaduct follows the system color scheme (default), forces a light theme, or forces a dark theme. Maps to AdwColorScheme.

key.name: notifications-on-refresh
key.summary: Show notification when new articles arrive
key.description: When enabled, viaduct emits a desktop notification summarizing the number of new articles received during each refresh cycle.

key.name: refresh-on-startup
key.description: When enabled, viaduct kicks off a refresh cycle automatically the first time the main window is shown after launch. Combine with the periodic-refresh interval below to keep articles fresh without ever pressing the refresh button manually.

key.name: refresh-interval-minutes
key.summary: Periodic refresh interval in minutes (0 = disabled)
key.description: How often viaduct kicks off a refresh cycle while the application is running. 0 disables periodic refresh. Wired through a glib timeout in the main window; pair with run-in-background to keep the timer firing while the main window is closed.

key.name: run-in-background
key.summary: Keep refreshing feeds after the window is closed
key.description: When enabled, viaduct hides the main window on close instead of quitting, and continues to run scheduled refresh cycles in the background. The first time you enable this, viaduct will ask the desktop for permission to run in the background via the org.freedesktop.portal.Background API.

key.name: retention-days
key.description: Articles older than this are pruned from the database (starred articles are exempt). Wired by the Phase 14 pruning engine.

key.name: font-ui
key.description: Optional font family name override for the GTK application chrome (sidebar, timeline, header bars, dialogs). Empty string means "follow the system default" (inherits whatever the user has set in GNOME tweaks / Adwaita's font preferences).

key.name: font-monospace
key.description: Optional font family name override for monospace content. Applied to `code` / `pre` elements rendered inside the article reading pane (WebKit) and to any monospace surfaces in the GTK chrome. Empty string means "follow system default".

key.name: font-serif
key.description: Optional font family name override for the article reading pane body text. Applied as a CSS body { font-family: … } rule layered after the active article theme's stylesheet. Empty string means "use the article theme's font" (which respects the bundled NNW theme typography choices). Named historically `font-serif` because early NNW themes defaulted to serif; the override applies regardless of family classification.

key.name: video-playback-mode
key.summary: How to play YouTube / Vimeo videos detected in articles
key.description: "in-pane" opens the video inside a sandboxed playback dialog backed by a fresh WebKit instance scoped to the embed origin (destroyed when the dialog closes so memory is reclaimed). "external" hands the URL off to the system default handler (xdg-open). "disabled" hides the play button entirely. The article-pane WebView's lockdown profile is unchanged in every mode — playback is opt-in and isolated.

key.name: article-font-scale
key.summary: Article reading font scale (percentage of base size)
key.description: Multiplier applied to the article body's base font size, expressed as a percentage. 100 = native theme size; 75 = 75% (smaller); 200 = double size (largest). Plumbed into the page wrapper as the `--article-font-scale` CSS custom property; the override stylesheet applies it via `font-size: calc(1em * var(--article-font-scale))` so theme rules expressed in `em` / `rem` scale proportionally.

key.name: article-line-height
key.description: Unitless CSS line-height for the article body, expressed as centi-units (150 = 1.5, 200 = 2.0 = double-spacing). Default 150 roughly matches most NNW themes' native line-height. Stored as an integer for the schema's `range` declaration; converted to a float and injected into the page wrapper's `--article-line-height` custom property.

key.name: article-theme
key.description: Which NetNewsWire-ported theme renders the article reading pane. "auto" pairs Sepia with light mode and Tiqoe Dark with dark mode; the other values pin a specific theme regardless of system color scheme. The selected theme's accent color also propagates across the GTK chrome (sidebar selection, focus rings, switches) so the entire window visually echoes the reading pane.
